guidance.detail: 10-Q MD&A does not restate forward revenue guidance; NVIDIA gives quarterly guidance in its earnings press release (8-K).

themes.evidence: Data Center revenue $75.2B, +92% YoY, on Blackwell 300 ramp and InfiniBand/Spectrum-X/NVLink demand.
Blackwell 300 is the majority of system shipments; Rubin platform expected to start shipping in H2 FY2027.
Customer AI buildout depends on data-center, energy, and capital availability; multi-year energy expansion could delay deployments.
China export controls: H200 licenses (Feb 2026) generated no revenue yet and carry a 25% US tariff; zero Data Center Hopper shipped to China vs $4.6B a year ago.
Rise of high-quality open-source foundation models could reduce demand if deployed on competitors' platforms.
Invested $18.6B in private companies and infrastructure funds in Q1 FY27 to cultivate the AI ecosystem (some indirectly buy NVIDIA).

risk_factors.summary: Supply-demand mismatch: long manufacturing lead times and uncertain capacity, plus demand-estimation error, have caused and could cause imbalances.
Customer concentration: a significant share of revenue comes from a limited number of partners/distributors; losing access to any could hurt revenue.
Export restrictions: complex laws including export controls on NVIDIA products (e.g., China) may adversely affect the business.
Competition could adversely impact market share and financial results.
If operating results fall below analyst/investor expectations, the stock price could decline.
